rationale: The citing paper uses Brown and Lahey (2015) for specific empirical claims about subgoal dynamics in debt repayment, not just as a generic background citation. It is cited in the theory section as evidence that goal-gradient effects appear in debt repayment, and later in Study 10 to justify the expectation that paying off one card can worsen repayment allocation choices. Those claims help motivate the debt-repayment application and interpretation of the citing paper's intervention results.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Brown and Lahey (2015) as prior empirical grounding for how subgoal success influences debt-repayment behavior, then builds on that setup by testing whether individual differences (PLRM) and reward-point design change those post-subgoal-success choices.
